source.title: Sample-efficient Cross-Entropy Method for Real-time Planning
source.venue: Proceedings of the 2020 Conference on Robot Learning

confidence_rationale: iCEM is directly about CEM as an MPC trajectory optimizer. Its repairs are conservative and proposal-side: temporally correlated action sequences, memory, momentum/refit variants, and executing evaluated best actions. It does not solve categorical first-action support, but it supports the design stance that CEM repairs should preserve proposal quality and instrumentation rather than adding a new motivational term.
failure_signatures: Independent per-timestep CEM samples can generate incoherent proposal surfaces that do not explore useful trajectory alternatives.
MPC variants that execute an unevaluated mean action can obscure which evaluated candidate actually carried the selected action support.

mapping.source_claim_statement: CEM can be improved for real-time model-predictive control by adding temporally correlated actions, memory, and related sample-efficiency modifications.
mapping.ree_translation: V3 support repair should stay at the proposal-distribution level. A conservative REE option is therefore support-preserving CEM instrumentation and minimal first-action support preservation, not a new drive or reward source. The paper also supports recording whether selected actions came from evaluated candidates rather than from an unevaluated mean.
mapping.mapping_caveat: iCEM targets continuous-control robotics and sample efficiency. It does not provide a direct categorical support floor across discrete first-action classes. REE's support-preserving flag remains an experimental local repair, not an iCEM implementation.
mapping.source_context: Model-based RL and MPC trajectory optimization, evaluated on MuJoCo and manipulation tasks with CEM-style planners.

confidence_components.notes: Strong fit to CEM/MPC proposal mechanics; moderate transfer risk because REE has discrete action-object decoding layered over continuous action-object proposals.
